prompt: Write a short poem (8–16 lines) about the feeling of finishing a long project. Any form you like.
response:
# Done

The last period lands like a stone
dropped into still water—
finally, finally, finally
rippling out to the edges.

My hands don't know what to do with themselves.
They hover, ridiculous birds,
forgetting the shape of the work
that held them for so long.

The desk looks different now.
The coffee rings, the scattered notes—
evidence of a person
who needed to make something,
and did.

I sit with the quiet
the way you sit with someone
after a long conversation.
Grateful. A little lost. Alive.
